categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue and EPS figures, profit margins, dividend announcements, buyback programs, and guidance vs expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, tech sector trends, macro news mentioning Alphabet, index moves, and cross-sector rotation narratives.

categories.label: Analyst Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings, and market capitalization milestone commentary.

categories.label: Antitrust, Regulatory & Legal
categories.description: DOJ search monopoly case, EU fines and DMA investigations, UK CMA probes, Play Store rulings, ad tech trials, privacy lawsuits, and global regulatory enforcement actions.

categories.label: AI & Product Innovation
categories.description: Gemini model launches and controversies, AlphaFold breakthroughs, custom chip development (Trillium, Axion), AI search features, DeepMind research, and Pixel hardware releases.

categories.label: Cloud & Enterprise Partnerships
categories.description: Google Cloud deals with enterprise customers, multi-year strategic alliances, AI integration partnerships, and competitive positioning against AWS and Azure.

categories.label: Infrastructure & Energy Investment
categories.description: Data center construction globally, nuclear and renewable energy procurement, clean energy agreements, and multi-billion dollar regional infrastructure commitments.

categories.label: Acquisitions & Strategic Investments
categories.description: Major acquisition targets (Wiz, HubSpot), talent acquisitions (Character.AI), minority investments (Flipkart, Anthropic), and portfolio adjustments.

categories.label: Leadership, Workforce & Corporate
categories.description: C-suite changes, layoffs and restructuring, executive departures, corporate governance actions, and organizational realignments.

dimensions.scale: 1=trivial mention in a roundup or minor operational detail with no revenue/margin implications, 10=direct large-scale impact on core revenue streams, margins, or total addressable market
1=outcome fully expected or in line with prior guidance and consensus, 10=outcome completely unexpected with no prior indication or dramatically divergent from expectations
1=affects the next few trading days only with no lasting structural consequence, 10=structural shift that reshapes business model, competitive position, or regulatory environment for years
1=neutral or purely informational tone with no positive or negative valence, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone conveying major triumph or severe threat
1=pure opinion, speculation, or vague commentary with no specific data, 10=hard quantitative data including specific financial figures, deal values, or measurable outcomes
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously good or bad with clear directional impact on the company
1=affects only this company with no broader implications, 5=sector-wide implications for tech or digital advertising, 10=macro or market-wide event affecting all equities
1=no effect on competitive positioning relative to peers, 10=major shift in competitive landscape altering market share dynamics or strategic positioning
1=no regulatory, legal, or government enforcement angle, 10=existential regulatory threat such as forced structural separation or business-line divestiture
1=no leadership, strategy, or organizational change angle, 10=major strategic pivot, key executive transition, or fundamental organizational restructuring
1=transient story that resolves within a single news cycle, 10=persistent story remaining relevant for months with ongoing developments and follow-up coverage
1=reinforces the prevailing investment thesis and existing market narrative, 10=fundamentally challenges or reframes the core thesis around the company
1=rehash or incremental update of a previously well-reported story with no new information, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously in the public domain
1=no financial results reported or results exactly match prior guidance and consensus expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss analyst expectations as described in the article

kept.materiality: Ranges from trivial roundup mentions and minor regional partnerships to DOJ structural breakup proposals and quarterly earnings driving $2 trillion valuation milestones
kept.surprise: Ranges from scheduled earnings conference call announcements and expected regulatory filings to the unexpected Gemini image generation pause, the surprise first-ever dividend announcement, and the DOJ requesting full breakup remedies
kept.temporal_horizon: Ranges from daily market roundups and transient sector rotation stories to multi-year antitrust proceedings, structural AI strategy shifts, and decade-long infrastructure investment commitments
kept.sentiment_strength: Ranges from neutral partnership announcements and corporate governance filings to strongly positive earnings beats with first-ever dividends and strongly negative DOJ breakup threats and billion-euro fines
kept.information_density: Ranges from vague market commentary and opinion pieces about AI competition to precise earnings releases with specific EPS, revenue, margin figures, and deal values in the billions
kept.directional_clarity: Ranges from highly ambiguous antitrust outcomes where remedies are uncertain and timelines unknown to unambiguous earnings beats, clear fine amounts, and definitive court rulings
kept.scope: Ranges from GOOGL-only events like CFO hire and Gemini pause to sector-wide AI regulation discussions and macro market selloffs affecting all equities
kept.competitive_impact: Ranges from routine cloud partnerships with no competitive shift to Character.AI talent acquisition in the AI race, Gemini vs OpenAI positioning, and nuclear energy deals reshaping data center economics
kept.regulatory_risk: Dominant theme for GOOGL with massive variation from articles with zero regulatory angle to DOJ proposing structural breakup, EU upholding multi-billion euro fines, and simultaneous antitrust actions across US, EU, UK, India, and Canada
kept.management_signal: Ranges from articles with no leadership dimension to hiring a new CFO from Eli Lilly, CEO Pichai addressing Gemini bias as unacceptable, major layoffs across multiple divisions, and reorganizing the Gemini team under DeepMind
kept.expected_duration: Ranges from one-day market roundups and minor product tests to antitrust proceedings spanning years with multiple appeals, and structural AI investment narratives persisting across the entire corpus period
kept.narrative_shift: Ranges from routine reinforcement of the AI-growth-and-cloud thesis to narrative-altering events like the DOJ breakup proposal challenging the conglomerate model, Q4 cloud miss undermining the AI monetization thesis, and the first-ever dividend signaling a new capital return posture
kept.repeatedness: High variation driven by the corpus structure: many stories have multiple update articles and press release duplicates scoring low, while first reports of the Wiz acquisition talks, Character.AI licensing deal, and Gemini delay score high
kept.financial_result_surprise: Clear variation across four earnings cycles: Q4 2023 cloud revenue missed expectations driving a sharp selloff, Q1 2024 beat expectations with surprise first dividend, and Q3 2024 beat on cloud acceleration — while the majority of non-earnings articles score at the floor

dropped.actionability: Most GOOGL articles cluster into two groups — directly tradeable news like earnings and deal rumors vs background regulatory and partnership stories — without sufficient gradation in between to be informative as a scoring dimension
dropped.controversy: GOOGL articles rarely show genuine investor division; antitrust risk and AI competition are broadly agreed-upon concerns where investors differ on magnitude rather than direction, producing little meaningful variation on a controversy scale
